name: Encrypted DNS (DoT/DoH/DoQ) Deployment Gaps
description: Detects missing or misconfigured encrypted DNS transport (DNS over TLS, DNS over HTTPS, DNS over QUIC). Per NIST SP 800-81r3 Section 4.2.1, organizations should deploy encrypted DNS to prevent eavesdropping and manipulation of DNS queries. Also detects rogue encrypted DNS endpoints that could bypass organizational security controls.
author: AC3 ScanForge
remediation: Deploy DNS over TLS (DoT) on TCP port 853 for internal resolvers. Deploy DNS over HTTPS (DoH) for browser-based resolution. Block unauthorized DoT traffic on TCP 853 via firewall. Detect and block rogue DoH endpoints using Response Policy Zones (RPZ) and firewall rules. Block direct queries to public DNS resolvers (8.8.8.8, 1.1.1.1) to prevent security control bypass. Use TLS 1.3 for all encrypted DNS connections. Consider DNS over QUIC (DoQ) on UDP 853 for improved performance.

intelligence.dfirRelevance: Unencrypted DNS traffic is a primary source of intelligence for network-level adversaries. DNS eavesdropping reveals browsing patterns, internal service names, and can be used for DNS hijacking attacks.

complianceMapping.controlTitle: Secure Name/Address Resolution Service (Recursive or Caching Resolver)
